name: Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño
name_original: Mirador da Terra Cha – circular por las Penas de Rodas – Tierra Llana del Miño
region_intro: Jogging around Outeiro De Rei offers access to a diverse natural landscape, characterized by the Miño River and its fluvial islands, including the Ínsua de San Roque. The region is part of the Terras do Miño Biosphere Reserve, featuring lush greenery and varied terrain suitable for running. Trails often follow riverbanks, pass through natural reserves, and offer views of unique geological formations like the Penas de Rodas. The municipality's commitment to sustainable development ensures well-maintained routes for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Outeiro De Rei The most popular jogging route is Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) moderate trail that takes about 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This route features expansive views of the Galician landscape and includes the unique Penas de Rodas rock formation. Another top favourite among local runners is Presa da Fábrica da Luz loop from Vilar, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.1 km) path. This route offers a scenic run through green landscapes, often following the course of the river. Local runners also love the Chanca Viaduct and Rato River – Río Rato Bike Path loop from P. F. Máximo (enfte. nº 48), an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through riverine scenery and past a viaduct, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around Outeiro De Rei is defined by the Miño River, its fluvial islands, and unique geological formations like the Penas de Rodas. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging runs. The routes in Outeiro De Rei are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1400 runners have used komoot to explore Outeiro De Rei's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Outeiro de Rei?
A: Outeiro de Rei offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 90 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running trails in Outeiro de Rei?
A: The running trails in Outeiro de Rei offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find approximately 15 easy routes, around 59 moderate trails, and about 17 more difficult options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Outeiro de Rei?
A: The running routes in Outeiro de Rei are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1400 runners have used komoot to discover the varied terrain, often praising the scenic river views and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any unique natural features or landmarks to see while jogging in Outeiro de Rei?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Outeiro de Rei feature the picturesque Miño River and its fluvial islands, such as the Ínsua de San Roque. You can also encounter unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penas de Rodas Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;, which offers distinctive scenery. The region is also part of the Terras do Miño Biosphere Reserve, providing lush green landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer river views or pass by water features?
A: Yes, the Miño River is a central element of the landscape in Outeiro de Rei. Many running paths, such as the "Sendero Insuas Do Miño", follow the riverbanks, offering beautiful views and passing by fluvial islands. You might also encounter the confluence of the Miño and Ladra rivers, which forms scenic waterfalls in Piago.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the running paths?
A: While Outeiro de Rei itself is rich in natural beauty, nearby Lugo offers significant historical attractions. You can find routes that pass close to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Walls of Lugo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4540840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Lugo&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/334304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of Lugo&lt;/a&gt;. One route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Walls of Lugo – Santiago Gate loop from Lugo&lt;/a&gt;, directly incorporates these historical elements.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Outeiro de Rei?
A: Yes, Outeiro de Rei has approximately 15 easy routes, many with minimal elevation gain, making them perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed jog. The "Sendero Insuas Do Miño" is an easy 1.8 km route along the Miño River, ideal for a leisurely run.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the area?
A: Outeiro de Rei offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991347308/mirador-da-terra-cha-circular-through-the-penas-de-rodas-flatland-of-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 10.1 km. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Lugo loop from Estadio Anxo Carro&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.9 km route.

Q: Are the jogging trails in Outeiro de Rei suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier and shorter routes along the Miño River are suitable for families, offering flat terrain and scenic views. The region's commitment to sustainable development ensures well-maintained paths. For a family outing with an added attraction, consider visiting Marcelle Natureza, which features walking trails within its zoological park.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Outeiro de Rei?
A: While specific dog policies can vary, many natural trails and river paths in Outeiro de Rei are generally welcoming to dogs, especially those in less populated areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Outeiro de Rei?
A: Outeiro de Rei, with its lush greenery and river systems, is pleasant for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in summer, the riverine paths can provide a refreshing experience. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some paths might be wetter.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for endurance training?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chanca Viaduct and Rato River – Río Rato Bike Path loop from P. F. Máximo (enfte. nº 48)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option at 13.2 km. The Ruta do Camiño do Miño, a moderate 13.2 km trail, also offers diverse landscapes and river views suitable for longer distances.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the running trails in Outeiro de Rei?
A: Parking availability varies by specific trailhead. For popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991347308/mirador-da-terra-cha-circular-through-the-penas-de-rodas-flatland-of-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find designated parking areas near the starting points. Many trails that begin in or near villages will have public parking options available.
tips: On a slope of Mount A Cruz de Paraños, elevated to 619 meters and with views of a large part of Terra Chá, is this magnificent set of rocks of unknown origin. For some, a natural formation created by molten magma and sculpted by wind and rain. For others, a creation of humanity due to its coincidence with the sunset on the summer solstice.
Viewpoint at an altitude of 600 meters with views of a large part of the Terra Chá region, in the province of Lugo.
